text: Corrupt. Hebrew, "scoffers," who provoke both God and men.
Rest; or bring him to hear reason. (Calmet)<hr>Septuagint, "a wise man shall rule nations; but the wicked being angry, is laughed at, and does not frighten."
Soul. They wish to protect the upright, Psalm 141:5.
Enlightener. Septuagint, "visitor," with punishment, as they seem to speak of palliated usury, which cannot escape God. See Proverbs 22:2.

Prophecy, by the urim, or by the mouth of prophets, who were in great power and estimation, 3 Kings 1:24., and 12:23., and 2 Paralipomenon 25:6., and 28:8. (Calmet)<hr>Septuagint, "There shall be none to explain the law to the impious nation." (Haydock)<hr>When pastors are wanting, all goes to ruin, 1 Corinthians 11:4. (Ven. Bede) (Calmet)
Amendment. St. Jerome (ad Evag.) says, after Thucydides: "Ignorance produces confidence, and learning fear."

Soul, and exposeth himself to death, though this was only inflicted for stealing a man, Exodus 21:16. The night-thief might also be killed in the fact, Exodus 22:2. But if the person who had stolen denied it on oath, he was put to death for perjury, Leviticus 5:1.

Fall. Human respects will not long preserve him from sin. (Calmet)<hr>Septuagint have a double version: "Those who fear and are ashamed of men, shall be thrown down. But he who confideth in the Lord, shall rejoice. Impiety overturneth man, while he who trusteth in the Lord shall be saved." (Grabe) (Haydock)
